source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, within circle $\odot O$, $OC \perp AB$. If $\angle BOC=40^\circ$, what is the value of $\angle OAB$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} In circle $\odot O$, $OA=OB$, \\
$\therefore \triangle AOB$ is an isosceles triangle,\\
$\because OC\perp AB$,\\
$\therefore \angle AOC=\angle BOC=40^\circ$,\\
$\therefore \angle AOB=80^\circ$,\\
$\therefore \angle OAB=(180^\circ-\angle AOB)\div 2=50^\circ$.\\
Thus, $\angle OAB=\boxed{50^\circ}$.

This is not a direct lookup; it requires combining at least two geometric facts before the final angle calculation

The provided answer ends with a specific boxed result: 50 degrees
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present
The solution structure is explicit enough for rule-based or symbolic post-checking
